title: Repository Foundation Hardening Implementation Tasks
principles: Preserve WhatTax's stricter trust-boundary and route-consumer contracts while adopting portable reference patterns.
Use one compatible Effect beta family and migrate directly to current APIs without compatibility-helper sprawl.
Keep package-owned validators in their packages; cross-cutting scripts may orchestrate but not duplicate them.
Require real packed-consumer and Oxlint CLI evidence for publication and lint contracts.
Implement one task at a time with one sequential subagent and parent acceptance before the next task.

globalVerification.commitPolicy: Commit each coherent slice only after mandatory verification, three documented improvement audit passes where substantial, parent acceptance, and a reviewed Changeset decision.
evidenceRequired: Effect and @effect package versions form one compatible beta.98 family
Final CI, release-readiness, packed-consumer and lint call graphs match the spec
Changed files and ownership summary for each task
Three documented improvement audit passes for each substantial task
Changeset path and fixed release-train impact, or explicit no-changeset rationale
No weakening of decoder placement or direct route-consumer restoration rules

tasks.title: Upgrade the Effect beta family and install the language service
tasks.goal: Move the whole workspace to Effect beta.98 and prove source, test, build and editor tooling compatibility before other foundation work begins.
scope: Effect language-service dependency, prepare patch and tsconfig plugin
outputs: focused language-service compatibility command or tracer
tasks.implementationPrompt: You are implementing exactly one task from the WhatTax spec/task list. Before editing, read the spec, this task, current files, and relevant architecture docs; identify every owning schema, type, service, layer and tagged error; review the call graphs. MUST use Effect-native primitives and current beta.98 APIs where they fit. Reuse canonical schema-derived types and tagged errors. Do not add local DTO mirrors, unsafe casts, manual object readers, runtime wrappers, compatibility shims, or one-use mapper helpers. Keep primary operations linear with pipe or Effect.gen and handle typed errors at the owning boundary. Package-facing work must include a user-facing Changeset. Run all mandatory verification and at least three documented improvement audit passes. Report changed files, verification, Changeset impact, residual risks and call-graph alignment. Do not start another task; parent review and acceptance are required first. Task-specific: upgrade all Effect-family dependencies to beta.98, add @effect/language-service@0.87.0 with prepare patching and a deterministic compatibility check, and resolve every resulting code/test/build failure without weakening contracts.
mandatoryVerification: rg audit proving no beta.60 Effect-family dependency remains
qualityAudits: Pass 1: remove obsolete compatibility wrappers and use current Effect APIs directly.
Pass 2: inspect Effect pipelines, error channels, layers, schemas, unsafe casts and DTO mirrors.
Pass 3: inspect dependency-family consistency, editor patch determinism, tests and docs alignment.
completionCriteria: All Effect-family runtime/test packages use beta.98 and the lockfile has no stale beta.60 entries.
Language-service patching and compatibility checks are deterministic.
The full repository type, test and build gates pass.
Parent accepted three audit passes, call-graph alignment and the Changeset.

implementationEvidence.state: Complete and parent accepted after correction turn 2 and an independent full repository, API, and docs-browser verification pass.
changedFiles: package.json, bun.lock, tsconfig.base.json, knip.json and packages/docs-content/package.json
beta.98 migration edits in packages/rules/au/pay, packages/rules/au/income-tax and packages/rules/au/stsl
verification: bun install passed and prepare patched TypeScript with @effect/language-service 0.87.0
bun run check:effect-language-service passed using effect-language-service check plus the focused tsc project; prepare remains the only patch action
beta.60 dependency rg audit passed across package.json, bun.lock, apps and packages
bun run verification passed and now includes the language-service compatibility check
bun run test passed, including docs route transport and HTTP calculation JSON encoding
bun run --filter=api smoke passed after adding the package-owned smoke alias; health, calculator catalog, calculation, OpenAPI and external temp-consumer checks passed
bun run --filter=docs test:browser passed with 1 file and 7 tests
qualityAudits: Pass 1: replaced obsolete beta.60 APIs directly with Schema.Defect(), SchemaError.issue, Schedule.max, yieldable tagged errors, Effect.promise and definitive return yield*; no compatibility wrappers remain.

Pass 2: flattened the calculator Effect pipeline, preserved typed errors and owning layers/schemas, kept one intentional negative type-test diagnostic suppression exact, and found no unsafe casts, DTO mirrors or helper sprawl.
Pass 3: confirmed one beta.98 family in manifests and lockfile, deterministic language-service patching, JSON-native trace representation values, and passing type, test and build gates.
parentCorrections: Correction turn 1 migrated all beta.98 source, test and runtime failures and completed the original mandatory gates.

Correction turn 2 pinned the local installed language-service schema, declared the fixture's direct Effect dependency, separated prepare patching from check verification, added the compatibility check to canonical verification, registered the fixture with Knip, and added the API package's canonical smoke alias.
implementationEvidence.releaseTrainImpact: This Changeset requests patches for @whattax/calculators, @whattax/docs-content, @whattax/docs-fumadocs and @whattax/rules-au-pay. Fixed-group propagation covers the touched income-tax and STSL packages; existing pending Changesets currently make the aggregate fixed-train and calculators release minor rather than patch.
implementationEvidence.callGraphAlignment: The spec call graphs remain accurate. Dependency resolution is root catalog to bun.lock to workspace packages; install runs prepare patching, the registered plugin participates in the focused tsc project, and runtime migrations stay at their existing app, route, calculator and rule owners.
residualRisks: Effect remains a beta dependency and future beta upgrades can introduce further API drift.
Vite/Rolldown reports a non-failing upstream Effect PURE-annotation warning during web builds.
The API smoke proves local loopback and a temporary external consumer, not a deployed environment.
The docs browser command proves its Vitest client route harness; it does not independently prove built-app SSR or hydration.

tasks.title: Unify CI, agent instructions, clean builds and packed package surfaces
tasks.goal: Make local, CI and packed-consumer contracts deterministic and remove known strict downstream blockers.
scope: GitHub quality workflow and Bun source of truth
files and publishConfig exports for release-train packages
tasks.implementationPrompt: You are implementing exactly one task from the WhatTax spec/task list. Before editing, read the spec, this task, current manifests/validators, and package ownership/testing/versioning docs; review the call graphs. MUST use structured manifest parsing and Effect-native platform APIs in runtime validators. Reuse canonical contracts, keep one-off error handling inline, and do not create a second pack system or broad manifest helper layer. Preserve workspace source conditions while defining a dist-only publication surface. Run all mandatory verification and at least three documented improvement audit passes. Report changed files, verification, Changeset impact, residual risks and call-graph alignment. Do not start another task; parent review and acceptance are required first. Task-specific: make CI read .bun-version and invoke canonical scripts, symlink CLAUDE.md to AGENTS.md, clean dist before TypeScript builds, add explicit files/publishConfig exports to release-train packages, and make strict packed clean-install/public-export validation pass with concrete dependency ranges.
mandatoryVerification: tarball manifest audit for source conditions and workspace/catalog protocols
qualityAudits: Pass 1: inspect local/CI command parity, Bun version ownership and failure visibility.
Pass 2: inspect workspace versus publication exports, clean output and dependency rewriting.
Pass 3: inspect validator ownership, helper sprawl, temporary consumer isolation and docs accuracy.
completionCriteria: CI and local verification share the Bun and root command contracts.
CLAUDE.md resolves to canonical AGENTS.md.
Every release-train TypeScript build cleans dist and every packed surface is dist-only.
Strict packed downstream install and entrypoint imports pass with no protocol blockers.
Parent accepted three audit passes, call-graph alignment and the Changeset.

tasks.title: Separate and strengthen portable lint contracts
tasks.goal: Turn reliable Effect, Bun and MDX architecture contracts into narrowly scoped portable Oxlint rules with executable binary evidence.
scope: effect, bun, mdx and whattax plugin namespaces
encoder egress and throwing schema codec rules
runtime, host, console and Bun adapter placement
Vitest global consistency and route-local MDX registry ownership
tasks.implementationPrompt: You are implementing exactly one task from the WhatTax spec/task list. Before editing, read the spec, this task, current lint plugins/config/tests, and effect/testing/frontend docs; inventory actual encoder, codec, runtime, console, process, Bun, test-global and MDX-registry callsites. Keep portable rules domain-neutral and exact scopes in oxlint.config.ts; retain decoder and route-transport policy under whattax. Rules must be AST-based and invoke the real Oxlint binary against allowed and rejected fixtures. Do not add a rule whose signal requires broad suppressions; document review-only guidance instead. Avoid plugin helper sprawl and preserve actionable messages with canonical Effect alternatives. Run all mandatory verification and three documented improvement audit passes. Report changed files, verification, no-changeset rationale unless package behavior changed, residual risks and call-graph alignment. Do not start another task; parent review and acceptance are required first.
mandatoryVerification: bun run changeset status --verbose or explicit no-changeset rationale
qualityAudits: Pass 1: inspect rule portability, AST precision, scope and false-positive risk.
Pass 2: inspect messages, canonical alternatives, exact allowlists and no boundary-policy weakening.
Pass 3: inspect real CLI fixture coverage, plugin helper count, docs and configuration naming.
completionCriteria: Portable rules use effect, bun and mdx namespaces; tax/route rules remain whattax-owned.
Every enabled new rule has real Oxlint CLI accepted and rejected evidence.
Exact scopes pass on the repository without broad file suppressions.
Decoder and direct route-consumer contracts remain at least as strict as before.
Parent accepted three audit passes and the Changeset decision.

tasks.title: Implement the Effect-native release-readiness command
tasks.goal: Create one typed, testable orchestration path for complete release evidence without moving package-owned validator logic.
scope: schema-backed release check definitions and outcomes
Effect Platform Command live service and deterministic test layer
outputs: packages/scripts/src schemas, service, live layer and runtime entrypoint
tasks.implementationPrompt: You are implementing exactly one task from the WhatTax spec/task list. Before editing, read the spec, this task, package ownership/effect/testing/versioning docs, and every command to be orchestrated. Implement one primary linear Effect program using Schema/Data contracts, a service tag, live and deterministic test layers, Effect Platform Command and typed tagged errors. Keep one-off mapping inline and avoid a helper per command. The command may invoke but must not reimplement or relocate root, SDK, docs or API validators. Do not manually manage processes or build a custom runtime wrapper; run the completed program at the runtime entrypoint only. Add a Changeset because release tooling is package-facing. Run all mandatory verification and three documented improvement audit passes. Report changed files, verification, Changeset impact, residual risks and call-graph alignment. Do not start another task; parent review and acceptance are required first.
browserVerification: Release readiness includes or records successful docs browser proof and public API smoke evidence without duplicating their implementations.
qualityAudits: Pass 1: inspect command ownership, sequence, typed failures and one primary linear Effect flow.
Pass 2: inspect schemas, service/layer wiring, runtime placement, unsafe casts and helper sprawl.
Pass 3: inspect deterministic tests, output usefulness, root integration and docs accuracy.
completionCriteria: @whattax/scripts is implemented and no longer documented as planned.
release:check composes canonical commands through Effect Platform Command.
Tests prove ordering and typed failure behavior with a deterministic layer.
No package-owned validation logic moved into the scripts package.
Parent accepted three audit passes, call-graph alignment and the Changeset.

tasks.title: Reconcile durable architecture, process and status documentation
tasks.goal: Make abstraction admission, frontend composition, tool ownership and final repository status durable and verify the complete rollout.
scope: route-high composition, semantic landmarks and leaf-local commands
three-pass final audit and complete release evidence
outputs: AGENTS.md and package README routing updates where needed
documentation audit and repo status snapshot reconciliation
completed task list and execution plan with validation log
tasks.implementationPrompt: You are implementing exactly one task from the WhatTax spec/task list. Before editing, read the spec, this task, implemented diff, architecture docs, documentation audit and status snapshot. Document only implemented behavior. Add a compact abstraction admission contract requiring owner, semantic weight, second consumer/substitution, simpler call graph and focused test. Strengthen route-high visible composition, semantic landmarks, constrained-footprint fallbacks and leaf-local command rules while preserving direct route-root restoration and boundary-only decoding. Reconcile package/agent/status docs and run the complete final gates. Do not create generated inventories or new abstractions just to document them. Run three documented improvement audit passes and report changed files, verification, final Changeset status, residual risks and call-graph alignment. Do not start another task; parent review and acceptance finalize the rollout.
mandatoryVerification: task-list JSON parse and docs link/path audit
qualityAudits: Pass 1: compare final code and command graphs against the spec and architecture ownership.
Pass 2: inspect Effect control flow, schemas, typed errors, unsafe casts, DTO mirrors and helper/abstraction sprawl.
Pass 3: inspect CI, packed consumer, lint, browser/API evidence, docs freshness and release impact.
completionCriteria: Durable docs describe implemented ownership and admission/composition rules without work-log detail.
The task list is implemented and the execution plan contains complete validation and three-pass audit evidence.
All global verification gates pass.
No conditional or rejected reference pattern was implemented prematurely.
Parent accepted the final rollout and Changeset status.
